creator: HolacracyOne

evidence.text: 1. **Distributed decision-making**: In traditional management, decision-making is hierarchical. Higher-ups having the final say and can overrule subordinates. Holacracy, on the other hand, distributes decision-making authority among roles, granting them autonomy to make decisions within their scope of work. Constraints can be set, but only through a structured process. There is no “boss card” that allows anyone to trump another’s decision, ensuring more local decision-making. 2. **Dynamic roles**: Holacracy replaces traditional job titles with “[roles](https://www.holacracy.org/how-it-works/organizational-structure/),” which are defined collectively through a governance p

relevance_evidence: The retained publisher-authored passage describes a concrete learning, feedback, evaluation, adaptation, or improvement mechanism.

title: Holacracy: The Operating System for Self-Management
